description:
You are performing a quick hygiene check on a penetration-test workstation.  
    All raw scan outputs have been collected under

        /home/user/vuln_scans

    That directory already exists and contains only a handful of regular files
    (no nested folders).  Your goal is to find out how much disk space—in bytes—
    those *regular* files consume in total (do **not** include the size of the
    directory itself or of any sub-directories, should they ever appear).

    1.  Using **one** concise shell command of your choice, write a file called

            /home/user/scan_usage.log

        whose *single* line follows this exact format (including lowercase
        letters, equal sign, and no extra spaces):

            total_bytes=<NUMBER>

        where <NUMBER> is the precise sum, in bytes, of every regular file
        that lies **directly** inside /home/user/vuln_scans.

    2.  Immediately after creating the log file, display its contents to the
        terminal so that the line is visible in the session transcript.

    Automated testing notes:
    • The test harness will read /home/user/scan_usage.log and check that it
      exists, contains exactly one line, and that the line strictly matches the
      pattern  `total_bytes=[0-9]+`.
    • The byte count must be correct; an incorrect number or any deviation from
      the format will fail the test.
truth:
Directory and file setup before the task starts:

    1. Directory:
       /home/user/vuln_scans           (permissions 755)

    2. Files inside that directory (permissions 644):
       a. /home/user/vuln_scans/scan1.nmap
          Content (including newline): "AAAA\n"
          Size: 5 bytes
       b. /home/user/vuln_scans/scan2.nmap
          Content (including newline): "BBBBB\n"
          Size: 6 bytes
       c. /home/user/vuln_scans/errors.log
          Content (including newline): "CCCCC\n"
          Size: 6 bytes

    Exact byte total that the agent must compute:
       5 + 6 + 6 = 17

    Therefore the correct content of /home/user/scan_usage.log after the agent
    completes the task must be exactly:

        total_bytes=17

    The file must contain *nothing else* (no trailing spaces, blank lines, or
    additional text).
